---
title: "Bora Kang"
subtitle: "Ph.D. in English"
image: images/BAB58528-B764-40DC-8037-FC4E760A8CB8.jpg

about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 10em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:bkang21@binghamton.edu"
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=_4tWMuwAAAAJ&hl=en"
---

## About

I am a scholar of Anglophone literature specializing in modern and contemporary British literature, film and media, visual studies, and cultural studies. I received my Ph.D. in English from Binghamton University. My research examines how literary and cultural forms shape affective experience across national belonging, collective memory, and cultural identity.

My work traces how affective configurations, expressed across the British novel and heritage media, have shaped Englishness and national identity over time. My current project develops this research into a monograph, *Affective Drift and Englishness from Woolf to Brexit: Literature, Film, and Media*.

This inquiry has since expanded toward affective experience among non-Western and marginalized subjects, tracing how emotion circulates unevenly within diasporic and postcolonial narratives. A parallel strand of my work examines how diverse media—including streaming platforms and physical sites like museums—mediate affective experiences and shape the social imaginaries that define contemporary life.

## Research & Teaching Interests

- Modern and Contemporary Anglophone Literature
- British Literature and Cultural Identity
- Emotion and Affect Theory
- Narrative and Seriality in Screen Media
- Visuality and Perception
- Heritage Media and Cultural Memory
